mission.title: Install and Configure Dify
mission.description: Dify is a production-ready platform for developing agentic workflows, designed for self-hosting on Kubernetes. It provides a robust framework for building AI applications with ease, but requires careful resource management and monitoring due to its complexity.

steps.title: Add the Dify Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Dify Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install Dify easily.

```bash

helm repo add dify https://charts.dify.ai

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Dify Helm repository.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Dify CRDs
steps.description:
Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) are required for Dify to function correctly. This step installs them into your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/langgenius/dify/main/deploy/crds.yaml

```

> This command installs the necessary CRDs for Dify.

steps.title: Install Dify using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the Dify application in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install dify dify/dify --namespace dify --create-namespace --version 0.12.0

```

> Replace 0.12.0 with the desired version if needed.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) to demonstrate how to use Dify.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.ai/v1
kind: DifyApp
metadata:
  name: example-dify-app
spec:
  description: Example Dify application
EOF

```

> This command creates an example Dify application.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.ai/v1
kind: DifyApp
metadata:
  name: example-dify-app
spec:
  description: Example Dify application
EOF

steps.title: Verify Dify installation
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Dify application and ensures it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n dify

kubectl get difyapp example-dify-app -n dify

```

> This command lists all pods in the Dify namespace to verify they are running.
> This command checks the status of the example Dify application.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Dify
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Dify application and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data associated with the Dify application.

```bash

helm uninstall dify --namespace dify

kubectl delete namespace dify

```

> This command uninstalls the Dify application.
> This command deletes the Dify namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Dify
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step will upgrade Dify to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get difyapp example-dify-app -n dify -o yaml > backup-example-dify-app.yaml

helm upgrade dify dify/dify --namespace dify --version 0.12.0

kubectl get pods -n dify

```

> Backup the current state of the example Dify application.
> Replace 0.12.0 with the desired version.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get difyapp example-dify-app -n dify -o yaml > backup-example-dify-app.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'DifyApp not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified DifyApp resource does not exist in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the DifyApp resource was created successfully and check the namespace.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes cluster may not be reachable or the kubeconfig is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check your kubeconfig file and ensure that the cluster is running.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application inside the pod is crashing due to misconfiguration or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n dify' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the Dify pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the resources available in your cluster or reduce the resource requests of the Dify deployment.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new workflow features and improved API performance.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old workflow syntax in favor of the new streamlined version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 0.11, update your workflow definitions to align with the new syntax.

resolution.summary: You should see the Dify application running with the status 'Running' and the example DifyApp resource in 'Ready' state when everything is working.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.ai/v1
kind: DifyApp
metadata:
  name: example-dify-app
spec:
  description: Example Dify application
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
